new_findings.title: research/codebase-analysis.md doesn't carry CSRF wiring breadcrumb under Auth section
new_findings.proposed_fix: 1-line breadcrumb: 'CSRF wiring: backend SessionMiddleware enforces double-submit on writes; frontend api-client.ts lifts auxd_csrf cookie into X-CSRF-Token header for every state-changing method. Header registered in backend CORS allow_headers.'

new_findings.title: spec.md NFR Security row (line 300) stale on 3 counts: (a) 'OAuth tokens encrypted at rest' (CR-001 dissolved Spotify); (b) 'bcrypt cost ≥12' (code is argon2 since T053 — Run #12 L2-034 carry-forward); (c) generic 'CSRF' doesn't pin T173 contract
new_findings.proposed_fix: Rewrite NFR Security row: drop OAuth clause; replace bcrypt with argon2 + validate_password_policy ≥12 chars; expand CSRF to 'double-submit (auxd_csrf cookie + X-CSRF-Token header) enforced by SessionMiddleware on writes; frontend api-client.ts lifts cookie → header on POST/PATCH/PUT/DELETE'.

new_findings.title: plan §16 + §17 + §18 don't cross-reference the 7 operator runbooks + audit docs shipped at S26 (docs/staging.md, docs/closed-beta-runbook.md, docs/launch.md, docs/launch-checklist.md, docs/security-review.md, docs/a11y-audit.md, docs/perf-audit.md)

new_findings.title: plan §4 doesn't document T173 CSRF header-lift contract (only generic 'CSRF protection via double-submit cookie pattern' at line 382)
new_findings.proposed_fix: Append ~10 lines under §4 or new §4.7 'CSRF wiring contract' covering: auxd_csrf cookie set semantics (SameSite=Lax + non-HttpOnly); frontend api-client.ts cookie reader + X-CSRF-Token header lift on state-changing methods; backend main.py CORS allow_headers includes X-CSRF-Token; SessionMiddleware enforces match (missing/mismatched → 403).

honest_assessment: Session 26 was the FINAL cluster — deliberately verify-and-ship work, not new features. The 9 §18 tasks shipped 7 operator runbooks + 8 audit/runbook doc files + 13 TC-E2E spec files + 9 a11y/perf test specs + 4 minimal code-fix touches (2 a11y empty-TabsContent fixes, 1 CSRF wiring fix, 1 design-polish transition class). The T173 OWASP review's HIGH-risk catch (CSRF wiring bug — frontend never lifted auxd_csrf cookie into X-CSRF-Token header; production state-changing requests would have 403'd) is itself the strongest validation of the sync-verify-and-audit gate cycle — caught BEFORE launch, fixed inline with 3 regression unit tests. The drift this run is small and almost entirely about whether plan.md and spec.md cross-reference the new audit-output doc files — not about feature gaps. Every Must-Have user story has implementation evidence + every NFR has a measurable signal + every TC-E2E ID has a spec file + every operator workflow has a runbook. The 8 inline edits proposed (1 research breadcrumb + 1 spec.md NFR Security rewrite + 1 spec.md NFR anchor batch + 3 plan.md edits + 2 tasks.md housekeeping touches) close the gap; post-application, the feature meets the Phase 9 release-readiness gate and is ready for M0 launch. The 5 long-standing carry-forwards (invite-mechanic + Paths-convention umbrella + US-A1 polish) remain user-deferred under their existing dispositions and do not block launch.
next_action: User reviews Run #13 findings + chooses disposition. Recommended: apply 8 inline edits in a single coordinated pass mirroring Runs #10 + #11 + #12. After application: feature is launch-ready per docs/launch-checklist.md + docs/launch.md. The 5 long-standing CFs (invite-mechanic + Paths convention + US-A1 polish) may either ship in a post-launch v1.x cleanup pass or remain deferred indefinitely under operator discretion. Launch execution itself is operator-driven per docs/launch.md T-72h → T+72h timeline. Sync-verify cadence after launch becomes retrospective (Phase 10 Retrospective) rather than feature-cluster-trigger.
